mnemonics: BAP (Bayesian Networks, Approximate Inference, Probabilistic Reasoning) - Think of a 'BAP' of knowledge for handling uncertainty.
keyPoints: Bayesian networks represent dependencies among random variables using a directed acyclic graph.
Probabilistic reasoning uses the axioms of probability to handle uncertain knowledge.
Approximate inference methods, like sampling, are used when exact inference is computationally intractable.
commonMistakes: Confusing prior probability with conditional probability (P(A) vs. P(A|B)).
Assuming all events are independent when they are not, leading to incorrect joint probability calculations.
Misinterpreting the direction of causality in a Bayesian network (the arc A->B does not always mean A causes B).
